Title: Abscisic Acid-Stress-Ripening Genes Involved in Plant Response to High Salinity and Water Deficit in Durum and Common Wheat

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: In this study, qRT-PCR primers (P6) amplify both TtASR-4A and TtASR-4B, so expression data represent combined transcript levels of the two homoeologs. Both genes share a simple 2-exon/1-intron structure with high sequence similarity (≈97.3%).
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TtASR-4A was identified in the Svevo genome as the homoeologous ASR gene on chromosome 4A corresponding to the previously isolated Mahmoudi TtASR1.
2. TtASR-4A (together with TtASR-4B) is constitutively expressed in leaves and roots and strongly salt-responsive in roots, with up-regulation in salt-tolerant and repression in salt-susceptible genotypes.
3. Under PEG-induced water deficit, TtASR-4A/4B expression shows mainly non-discriminatory repression in roots of both tolerant and susceptible genotypes.
4. Root expression level at 72 h of salt stress is suggested as a functional parameter for distinguishing salt tolerance and for developing ASR-based markers in durum wheat.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher root expression at 72 h under prolonged high salinity is associated with the salt-tolerant genotype, whereas repression is observed in the susceptible genotype; under PEG-induced water deficit, TtASR-4A/4B expression does not distinguish drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ASR (abscisic acid-stress-ripening) protein containing the conserved ABA_WDS (abscisic acid-water-deficit stress) domain (PF02496)
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves and roots without stress. Under 200 mM NaCl, using primers amplifying both TtASR-4A and TtASR-4B, transcripts are a-specifically induced in leaves of both genotypes (≈3.1–3.8-fold at 72 h) but show contrasting regulation in roots: down-regulated in susceptible HmiraK11835S (≈0.6–0.8 of control at 72 h) and up-regulated in tolerant HmiraK11857T (≈3-fold at 72 h). Under 15% PEG, expression is largely unchanged at 72 h: in roots both genotypes are slightly repressed (≈0.8 vs 0.5 of control in susceptible vs tolerant), with only transient early changes; leaves show mainly non-significant variation at 72 h. Root expression is generally higher than in leaves.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: TRITD4Av1G160700 (splicing form TRITD4Av1G160700.1); corresponds to Mahmoudi TtASR1 GenBank KX660742/KX660744
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Durum wheat cv. Svevo reference genome (Maccaferri et al. 2019)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl (salt stress) and 15% PEG 6000 (osmotic/water-deficit stress) applied to 10-day-old seedlings; sampling at 0, 6, 24, 72 h post-treatment.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; salt and drought tolerance in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (high salinity and water deficit/drought)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 200 mM NaCl, leaves reach ≈3.1-fold (susceptible) and 3.8-fold (tolerant) induction at 72 h; roots show ≈0.6–0.8-fold (susceptible) vs ≈3-fold (tolerant) expression at 72 h. Under 15% PEG, roots at 72 h show ≈0.8 (susceptible) vs ≈0.5 (tolerant) of control; leaf expression at 72 h is close to control in both genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tunisian durum wheat genotypes HmiraK11835S (salt/drought-susceptible) and HmiraK11857T (salt/drought-tolerant) seedlings; leaves and roots sampled at 0, 6, 24, 72 h after stress
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLAST searches against Svevo genome; MUSCLE multiple sequence alignment; gene and protein structure comparison; primer design in conserved exons; SYBR Green qRT-PCR on Actin-normalized cDNA; NaCl and PEG stress treatments on seedlings; densitometric analysis of RT-PCR products; statistical analysis by Student’s t-test.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Time-course qRT-PCR expression profiling under 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG in contrasting salt/drought-tolerant and -susceptible genotypes
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: In silico BLAST of previously isolated TtASR1 gene against the Svevo genome to identify homoeologous ASR genes on chromosomes 4A and 4B; candidate-gene based reverse genetics
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Root expression of TtASR-4A/4B at 72 h after 200 mM NaCl is proposed as a discriminating parameter between salt-tolerant and salt-susceptible durum wheat genotypes (≈3 vs 0.6-fold), supporting TtASR-4A as part of a robust candidate-gene set for abiotic stress resistance and as a target for functional marker development in durum wheat breeding.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Expression profiling could not separate TtASR-4B from TtASR-4A because of a single primer pair (P6) amplifying both homoeologs; conclusions on regulation are therefore for the combined TtASR-4A/4B signal.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TtASR-4B was identified as the 4B homoeolog of the durum wheat ASR gene family, highly similar (≈97.3%) to TtASR-4A and sharing a simple 2-exon/1-intron structure.
2. Combined TtASR-4A/4B transcripts are differentially regulated by high salinity in roots of tolerant vs susceptible genotypes, with up-regulation in tolerant and repression in susceptible plants.
3. Under PEG-induced water deficit, TtASR-4A/4B are weakly repressed in roots of both genotypes and do not clearly distinguish drought tolerance.
4. The contrasting salt-induced root expression pattern supports inclusion of TtASR-4B in ASR-based markers for salt-tolerance selection in durum wheat.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Like TtASR-4A, TtASR-4B shows salt-responsive expression in roots, with higher transcript accumulation in the salt-tolerant genotype and repression in the susceptible one, supporting a role in root-mediated adaptation to high salinity; PEG-induced drought response is weak and not clearly associated with drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ASR (abscisic acid-stress-ripening) protein family; simple 2-exon/1-intron gene structure typical of ASR genes
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves and roots without stress. Using primers that co-amplify TtASR-4A and TtASR-4B, transcripts are a-specifically induced in leaves under 200 mM NaCl in both susceptible and tolerant genotypes (≈3.1–3.8-fold at 72 h). In roots under NaCl, expression is down-regulated in susceptible HmiraK11835S (≈0.6–0.8 of control) and up-regulated in tolerant HmiraK11857T (≈3-fold) at 72 h. Under 15% PEG, both homoeologs show mainly weak repression in roots (≈0.8 vs 0.5 of control at 72 h in susceptible vs tolerant) and little change in leaves at 72 h.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: Durum wheat cv. Svevo reference genome (Maccaferri et al. 2019)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl (salt stress) and 15% PEG 6000 (osmotic/water-deficit stress) applied to 10-day-old seedlings; sampling at 0, 6, 24, 72 h post-treatment.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; salt and drought tolerance in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (high salinity and water deficit/drought)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 200 mM NaCl, combined TtASR-4A/4B root expression at 72 h is ≈3-fold in tolerant HmiraK11857T vs ≈0.6–0.8-fold in susceptible HmiraK11835S; leaves show ≈3.1–3.8-fold induction in both genotypes. Under 15% PEG, roots at 72 h show ≈0.8 vs ≈0.5 of control (susceptible vs tolerant).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tunisian durum wheat genotypes HmiraK11835S (salt/drought-susceptible) and HmiraK11857T (salt/drought-tolerant) seedlings; leaves and roots sampled at 0, 6, 24, 72 h after stress
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLAST and MUSCLE alignment to define homoeologous ASR genes; primer design across conserved exons; SYBR Green qRT-PCR normalized to Actin; NaCl and PEG stress treatments on seedlings; densitometric RT-PCR and statistical analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Time-course qRT-PCR expression profiling under 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG in contrasting salt/drought-tolerant and -susceptible genotypes using a primer pair that co-amplifies TtASR-4A and TtASR-4B.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homoeologous ASR gene on chromosome 4B identified by BLAST of Mahmoudi TtASR1 against the Svevo genome and comparison of ASR sequences on chromosomes 4A and 4B.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Combined root expression of TtASR-4A/4B at 72 h after salt treatment discriminates salt-tolerant from salt-susceptible durum wheat genotypes, making TtASR-4B together with TtASR-4A a promising component of ASR-based functional markers for selection of salt-tolerant durum wheat lines.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaASR-4A differs structurally from TaASR-4B/4D by having three exons and two introns, but its stress-regulated root expression pattern is similar to that of the 4B/4D homoeologs in tolerant vs susceptible genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaASR-4A was identified as a group-4A homoeologous ASR gene in common wheat with a distinct 3-exon/2-intron structure.
2. TaASR-4A is constitutively expressed and shows higher basal expression in roots than leaves.
3. Under both high salinity and PEG-induced drought, TaASR-4A is strongly up-regulated in roots of tolerant Ta001T but repressed in roots of susceptible Ta002S, while leaf responses are weak or non-discriminatory.
4. Root expression levels at 72 h of stress provide a clear molecular parameter to differentiate tolerant vs susceptible genotypes, highlighting TaASR-4A as a strong candidate for functional marker development.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root expression of TaASR-4A at 72 h under both high salinity and water deficit is strongly induced in the tolerant genotype and repressed in the susceptible one, making TaASR-4A expression a clear molecular correlate of salt and drought tolerance in common wheat.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ASR (abscisic acid-stress-ripening) protein family, characterized by the conserved ABA_WDS domain typical of ASR proteins
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves and roots without stress, with higher baseline expression in roots than leaves. Under 200 mM NaCl, leaf expression shows mainly a-speciﬁc variation, with final 72 h levels close to control in both Ta001T and Ta002S; root expression diverges, being up-regulated in tolerant Ta001T (≈2.6-fold at 72 h) and down-regulated in susceptible Ta002S (≈0.4-fold). Under 15% PEG, leaves of both genotypes show repression to similar levels (≈0.2–0.3 of control at 72 h), whereas roots again show contrasting regulation: strong up-regulation in tolerant Ta001T (≈5-fold at 72 h) and repression in susceptible Ta002S (≈0.5-fold). Root expression is consistently much higher than leaf expression, especially in the tolerant genotype.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring reference genome (Ensembl Plants)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl (salt stress) and 15% PEG 6000 (water-deficit stress), applied to 10-day-old seedlings; expression monitored at 0, 6, 24, 72 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; salt and drought tolerance in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (salt stress and PEG-induced drought/water deficit)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: High salinity (200 mM NaCl): at 72 h, TaASR-4A root expression is ≈2.6-fold of control in tolerant Ta001T vs ≈0.4-fold in susceptible Ta002S; leaf expression remains close to 1-fold in both genotypes. Water deficit (15% PEG): at 72 h, TaASR-4A root expression is ≈5-fold in Ta001T vs ≈0.5-fold in Ta002S; leaves are repressed to ≈0.2–0.3 of control in both genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tunisian common wheat genotypes Ta001T (salt/drought-tolerant) and Ta002S (salt/drought-susceptible) seedlings; leaves and roots sampled at 0, 6, 24, 72 h after stress
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLAST and multi-alignment of TaASR homoeologs on group 4 chromosomes; primer design for genome-specific (4A) amplification; SYBR Green qRT-PCR normalized to Actin; NaCl and PEG treatments on seedlings; time-course sampling; heat-map visualization; Student’s t-test for differential expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TaASR-4A-specific qRT-PCR (primer pair P1) time-course expression analysis under 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG in tolerant and susceptible common wheat genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Homoeologous ASR genes TaASR-4A, TaASR-4B, and TaASR-4D identified by BLAST of the durum TtASR1 sequence against the Chinese Spring genome and multi-alignment of group 4 ASR loci.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Root TaASR-4A expression at 72 h after NaCl or PEG treatment robustly distinguishes tolerant from susceptible common wheat genotypes, supporting TaASR-4A as a key candidate gene for abiotic stress resistance and as a functional marker target for selecting salt- and drought-tolerant wheat in breeding programs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Expression data for TaASR-4B and TaASR-4D cannot be distinguished in this study because primer pair P2 amplifies both homoeologs; all quantitative results refer to their combined transcript levels.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaASR-4B was identified as a 4B homoeolog of the ASR gene family in common wheat, sharing a conserved 2-exon/1-intron structure with TaASR-4D.
2. Combined TaASR-4B/4D transcripts show higher basal expression in roots than leaves, particularly in tolerant genotypes.
3. Under both high salinity and PEG-induced drought, TaASR-4B/4D are up-regulated in roots of tolerant Ta001T and repressed in roots of susceptible Ta002S, while leaves are generally repressed in both genotypes.
4. The contrasting root expression responses at 72 h of stress make TaASR-4B/4D valuable candidates for functional markers associated with salt and drought tolerance in common wheat.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Combined TaASR-4B/4D root expression at 72 h is strongly induced in the tolerant genotype and repressed in the susceptible one under both high salinity and water deficit, providing a clear molecular signature of stress tolerance in common wheat.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ASR (abscisic acid-stress-ripening) protein family with conserved gene structure of 2 exons separated by 1 intron
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves and roots, with higher baseline expression in roots, especially in the tolerant genotype. Using primer pair P2 that co-amplifies TaASR-4B/4D, under 200 mM NaCl leaf expression shows little discriminative change, with final strong repression at 72 h in both genotypes (≈0.26 and 0.36 of control in susceptible and tolerant, respectively). In roots under NaCl, expression diverges sharply: up-regulated in tolerant Ta001T (≈1.8-fold at 72 h) and repressed in susceptible Ta002S (≈0.5-fold). Under 15% PEG, leaves of both genotypes are down-regulated at 72 h (≈0.3 vs 0.5 of control in susceptible vs tolerant). In roots under PEG, TaASR-4B/4D are strongly induced in the tolerant line (≈5-fold at 72 h) and repressed in the susceptible one (≈0.3-fold).
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring reference genome (Ensembl Plants)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG 6000 applied to 10-day-old seedlings; expression monitored at 0, 6, 24, 72 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; salt and drought tolerance in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (salt stress and PEG-induced drought/water deficit)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: High salinity (200 mM NaCl): at 72 h, combined TaASR-4B/4D root expression is ≈1.8-fold of control in tolerant Ta001T vs ≈0.5-fold in susceptible Ta002S; leaf expression is reduced to ≈0.26 (susceptible) and 0.36 (tolerant) of control. Water deficit (15% PEG): at 72 h, root expression reaches ≈5-fold in Ta001T vs ≈0.3-fold in Ta002S; leaves fall to ≈0.3 (susceptible) and 0.5 (tolerant) of control.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tunisian common wheat genotypes Ta001T (salt/drought-tolerant) and Ta002S (salt/drought-susceptible) seedlings; leaves and roots sampled at 0, 6, 24, 72 h after stress
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Identification by BLAST and multi-alignment; design of P2 primers targeting conserved regions of TaASR-4B and TaASR-4D; SYBR Green qRT-PCR normalized to Actin; NaCl and PEG stress treatments; time-course expression analysis; heat-map visualization and statistical testing.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling using primer pair P2 that co-amplifies TaASR-4B and TaASR-4D under 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG in tolerant and susceptible common wheat genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a 4B homoeologous ASR gene by BLAST of durum TtASR1 against the Chinese Spring genome and multi-alignment of TaASR genes on chromosomes 4A, 4B, 4D.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Root expression of TaASR-4B/4D at 72 h under NaCl and PEG effectively separates tolerant from susceptible common wheat genotypes, supporting TaASR-4B as a component of ASR-based functional markers for selecting salt- and drought-tolerant lines in breeding programs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaASR-4D is almost identical in sequence and gene structure to TaASR-4B (≈99.3% similarity), and their expression cannot be distinguished experimentally in this work because the same P2 primer pair amplifies both homoeologs; conclusions are based on their combined expression profile.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaASR-4D was identified as a 4D homoeolog of the wheat ASR gene family with a conserved 2-exon/1-intron gene structure.
2. In combination with TaASR-4B, TaASR-4D shows higher expression in roots than leaves and generally higher expression in hexaploid than tetraploid wheat.
3. Combined TaASR-4B/4D transcripts are up-regulated in roots of tolerant Ta001T and repressed in roots of susceptible Ta002S under both high salinity and PEG-induced drought, while leaves are repressed in both genotypes.
4. This contrasting root expression pattern supports inclusion of TaASR-4D, together with TaASR-4B, in ASR-based molecular tools for selecting stress-tolerant common wheat.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Combined TaASR-4B/4D root expression is strongly induced in the tolerant genotype and repressed in the susceptible genotype under both salt and drought, indicating that TaASR-4D, together with TaASR-4B, contributes to enhanced root-level adaptation to abiotic stress in common wheat.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ASR (abscisic acid-stress-ripening) protein family with conserved 2-exon/1-intron structure similar to TaASR-4B
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in leaves and roots, with higher basal expression in roots and stronger overall expression in hexaploid than tetraploid wheat. In this study, TaASR-4D expression is monitored together with TaASR-4B: leaves show strong repression at 72 h under both NaCl and PEG in both genotypes, whereas roots exhibit up-regulation in tolerant Ta001T and repression in susceptible Ta002S under both stresses (≈1.8 vs 0.5-fold under NaCl; ≈5 vs 0.3-fold under PEG at 72 h).
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring reference genome (Ensembl Plants)
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG 6000 applied to 10-day-old seedlings; sampling at 0, 6, 24, 72 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; salt and drought tolerance in wheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress response (salt stress and PEG-induced drought/water deficit)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: At 72 h of 200 mM NaCl, combined TaASR-4B/4D root expression is ≈1.8-fold of control in tolerant Ta001T vs ≈0.5-fold in susceptible Ta002S; under 15% PEG, root expression is ≈5-fold in Ta001T vs ≈0.3-fold in Ta002S, while leaves are suppressed to ≈0.26–0.36 (NaCl) and ≈0.3–0.5 (PEG) of control.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tunisian common wheat genotypes Ta001T (salt/drought-tolerant) and Ta002S (salt/drought-susceptible) seedlings; leaves and roots sampled at 0, 6, 24, 72 h after stress
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLAST-based gene identification; multi-sequence alignment; design of P2 primers targeting conserved regions of TaASR-4B/4D; SYBR Green qRT-PCR normalized to Actin; NaCl and PEG stress treatments; time-course expression analysis with statistical evaluation.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling with primer pair P2 that co-amplifies TaASR-4B and TaASR-4D under 200 mM NaCl and 15% PEG in tolerant and susceptible common wheat genotypes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a 4D homoeologous ASR gene by BLAST of durum TtASR1 against the Chinese Spring genome and alignment of TaASR loci on group 4 chromosomes.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because combined TaASR-4B/4D root expression at 72 h under salt and drought clearly differentiates tolerant from susceptible genotypes, TaASR-4D is part of a multi-copy ASR gene set proposed as robust candidates for abiotic stress resistance and as targets for functional marker development in common wheat breeding.
